codeReview.summary: The panel wiring and collapse/empty-state behavior are largely correct, but the SIGN row detail extraction does not match real runtime `pending_operations` context shape, so required sign message previews are missing in practice.

issues.description: `extractMessagePreview` only reads `context.message_hex_32` / `context.message_hex`, but real runtime SIGN pending ops serialize context as nested `SignSession` data (e.g. `context.SignSession.session.hashes`) rather than those top-level fields. Result: SIGN rows omit the required first-10-hex message preview from real `pending_operations`, violating VAL-APPROVALS-002 and the feature contract. Evidence: the mission runtime capture shows `context.SignSession` in `/Users/plebdev/.factory/missions/b48100dd-0e6c-4a7c-90a3-f12e61d1c3c4/evidence/m1-ops/r2-g2-failure-paused/06-sign-dispatch-state.json` lines 116-117 and hash bytes at line 302.

The test suite validates SIGN message preview using a synthetic context shape (`{ message_hex_32: ... }`) that does not reflect observed runtime snapshots, so it misses the production parsing failure and gives false confidence.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission policy and skill guidance conflict on agent-browser for UI changes, which caused procedure ambiguity for this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS says agent-browser is mandatory for UI validation (`/Users/plebdev/.factory/missions/b48100dd-0e6c-4a7c-90a3-f12e61d1c3c4/AGENTS.md:84`), while `ui-screen-worker` allows skipping agent-browser for data-wiring fixes (`.factory/skills/ui-screen-worker/SKILL.md:23`). The worker marked `followedProcedure: false` and skipped agent-browser in handoff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Runtime `pending_operations.context` schema for SIGN is not captured in shared docs/types strongly enough, which likely contributed to incorrect UI parsing and unrealistic tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `PendingOperation.context` is typed as `unknown` (`src/lib/bifrost/types.ts:324`), and architecture docs do not spell out SIGN context payload fields. The implementation assumed top-level `message_hex_32` (`src/screens/DashboardScreen/panels/PendingApprovalsPanel.tsx:82-85`), but captured runtime evidence shows nested `SignSession` (`.../evidence/m1-ops/r2-g2-failure-paused/06-sign-dispatch-state.json:116-117`).

summary: Review result: FAIL. The feature mostly wires the panel correctly, but SIGN message preview extraction is incompatible with real runtime context, so contract-required sign detail rendering is not reliably met. Report saved at the required scrutiny review path.
